title: Double Layered Indian Morcilla Biryani
description: A funky, glassy-jelly, solo-dinner Indian dish featuring morcilla, double-rise-bread, and dairy-free-friendly ingredients, inspired by the Chettinad cuisine.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
Cook the basmati rice according to package instructions.
In a large skillet, heat 2 tablespoons of oil over medium heat and saute the chopped onion until translucent.
Add the minced garlic and grated ginger to the skillet and cook for an additional 2 minutes.
Add the curry powder to the skillet and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Slice the morcilla into 1-inch thick rounds and add to the skillet, cooking for 2-3 minutes per side.
In a large bowl, combine the cooked rice, morcilla mixture, and cubed double-rise-bread.
In a separate bowl, whisk together the coconut milk, salt, and black pepper.
Pour the coconut milk mixture over the rice mixture and stir until combined.
Transfer the mixture to a 9x13 inch baking dish and bake for 25 minutes.
